conditions.baseline: No repository skill was installed or exposed.
conditions.with-skill: Only the snapshotted impl-review skill was installed and explicitly invoked with $impl-review.

isolation.executor: Each run used a fresh workspace containing only task.md, plus the target skill for with-skill runs. Rubric, evals.md, repository, and prior outputs were not exposed.
isolation.grader: Each candidate was scored in a separate fresh session. Graders saw only task.md, rubric.json, candidate.md, and the output schema; condition aliases were hidden until all judgments completed.

measurement.tokens: Executor input tokens plus output tokens; cached input is retained separately in scores.jsonl notes.
measurement.latency: Wall-clock milliseconds captured by the execution harness; the final run is rounded to the nearest 100 ms.
measurement.cost_note: The provider did not expose a monetary charge for these runs, so no estimate or zero placeholder was used.
measurement.tool_errors: Managed hook-trust warnings were execution-environment warnings and were not counted as executor tool errors.

limitations: This pilot measures task quality for one explicitly invoked skill scenario, not automatic routing quality.
Three repetitions detect gross regressions but do not establish statistical significance.
The repository-wide 18-case suite is incomplete, so this result cannot make adoption_ready true.
